title: The One With The Sonogram At The End
weakest_section: 14:35-16:00 range with doctor's office setup having fewer laughs

jokes.text: Everything you need to know is in that first kiss. / Yeah, I think, for us... kissing is pretty much like an opening act. I mean, it's like the stand-up comedian you have to sit through... before Pink Floyd comes out.
jokes.setup: Women saying kissing is crucial and tells you everything
jokes.punchline: Men comparing kissing to a stand-up act before the main event (Pink Floyd)
jokes.explanation: Clever extended metaphor that perfectly captures the male perspective on foreplay vs. sex. Well-structured analogy with specific cultural reference.

jokes.text: See, the problem is, though... after the concert's over, no matter how great the show was... you girls are always looking for the comedian again.
jokes.setup: Continuing the concert metaphor about sex and foreplay
jokes.punchline: Women want cuddling/intimacy after sex (looking for the comedian again)
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the metaphor that feels natural for Joey's character perspective.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, word of advice: Bring back the comedian. Otherwise, you're gonna find yourself... sitting at home listening to that album alone.
jokes.setup: Men complaining about women wanting intimacy after sex
jokes.punchline: Rachel's threat using the same metaphor - no aftercare means no sex at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback that uses the established metaphor to flip the power dynamic. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Are we still talking about sex?
jokes.setup: Extended concert metaphor that became increasingly elaborate
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent confusion about what they're discussing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her innocent directness cuts through the elaborate metaphor. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: He's out banging other women over the head with a club... while she sits at home trying to get the mastodon smell out of the carpet.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if a caveman display looks angry
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's modern relationship analysis applied to cave people
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe absurdism - applying modern relationship problems to prehistoric times. Creative and character-specific.

jokes.text: Okay, they have issues like: 'Gee, that glacier's getting kind of close.' See?
jokes.setup: Phoebe giving cave people modern relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Ross's literal, scientific correction about what cave people would actually worry about
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's tendency to be overly literal and scientific.

jokes.text: How about I'll catch up with you in the Ice Age?
jokes.setup: Spotting his ex-wife Carol at the museum
jokes.punchline: Museum pun as Ross tries to escape awkward encounter
jokes.explanation: Weak museum pun that feels forced. Character-appropriate nervousness but not particularly funny.

jokes.text: ...stands erect...
jokes.setup: Ross trying to compliment Carol at the museum
jokes.punchline: Unintentional sexual innuendo in awkward conversation with lesbian ex-wife
jokes.explanation: Basic accidental innuendo joke. Gets a reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: A lesbian? Well... You never know.
jokes.setup: Ross asking if Carol is still a lesbian
jokes.punchline: Carol's noncommittal response to obvious question
jokes.explanation: Mild awkwardness humor from an obviously uncomfortable situation.

jokes.text: Oh. Looks like she didn't leave in such a hurry after all.
jokes.setup: Ross announces Carol is pregnant
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic observation about the pregnancy timing
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler-style sarcasm with solid timing after the pregnancy reveal.

jokes.text: I think this is the episode of Three's Company... where's there's some kind of misunderstanding. / Then I've already seen this one.
jokes.setup: Chandler comparing their situation to Three's Company
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact response about having seen it
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about sitcom formula with perfect Phoebe delivery. Self-aware without being too clever.

jokes.text: Yeah, sorry, the swallowing slowed me down.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Joey is done with food
jokes.punchline: Joey's TMI explanation about his eating process
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey character moment - gross but endearing. Not particularly clever but very in-character.

jokes.text: See, I wrote a note to myself, then I didn't need it. So I balled it up and now I wish I was dead.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about a ball of paper
jokes.punchline: Monica's dramatic escalation from mundane explanation to death wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica neurosis - mundane situation escalated to existential crisis. Good structure and character work.

jokes.text: Yes, we know how cruel a parent can be... about the flatness of a child's pillow.
jokes.setup: Monica worrying about giving parents ammunition
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic take on pillow-fluffing as serious parental criticism
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler sarcasm that highlights Monica's overthinking about her parents.

jokes.text: I mean, you're, like, all chaotic and twirly, you know? Not in a good way.
jokes.setup: Monica acting nervous about parents visiting
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's specific description with helpful clarification
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe observation with her typical blunt honesty and unique word choice.

jokes.text: You see, he's the prince. Apparently they had some big ceremony before I was born.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why Ross is the favorite child
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic description of birth order as royal ceremony
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry humor with Monica's bitter sarcasm about parental favoritism.

jokes.text: Ew, ew, ew. / What? / Ugly Naked Guy got a ThighMaster.
jokes.setup: Monica's disgusted reaction looking out the window
jokes.punchline: The visual of their neighbor using exercise equipment naked
jokes.explanation: Establishes recurring Ugly Naked Guy gag. Visual humor that works for broad sitcom audience.

jokes.text: Hi, Barry, remember me? I'm the girl... that stomped on your heart in front of your entire family
jokes.setup: Rachel rehearsing what to say to her ex-fiancé
jokes.punchline: Her brutally honest self-description of leaving him at the altar
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's guilt and self-awareness about her actions.

jokes.text: When did you have it on last? / Doy. Probably right before she lost it.
jokes.setup: Monica asking logical question about lost ring
jokes.punchline: Joey's sarcastic non-helpful response
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm joke. Joey being unhelpfully obvious.

jokes.text: You don't get a lot of 'doy' these days.
jokes.setup: Joey saying 'doy' as sarcastic response
jokes.punchline: Chandler's observation about the rarity of that particular slang
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler meta-observation about language/slang usage. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Oh, but look how straight those noodles are.
jokes.setup: Rachel feeding engagement ring to Monica's date's dog
jokes.punchline: Her pathetic attempt to find a silver lining in the disaster
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Rachel's desperate optimism in face of disaster.

jokes.text: Monica, you know that's not how you look for an engagement ring in a lasagna.
jokes.setup: Monica presumably digging through lasagna looking for ring
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction about proper ring-searching technique
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe line - treating absurd situation as if there's a proper protocol. Good visual comedy setup.

jokes.text: Oh, I found it.
jokes.setup: Ross announcing Carol's pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Monica's oblivious announcement about finding the ring at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic timing - trivial good news interrupting major life announcement. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Kind of puts that whole pillow thing in perspective, huh, Mon?
jokes.setup: Ross's pregnancy news after Monica's neurotic pillow-fluffing
jokes.punchline: Joey pointing out how trivial Monica's earlier worry was
jokes.explanation: Good callback that provides perspective on Monica's earlier neurosis.

jokes.text: She is so great. I miss her.
jokes.setup: Ross describing how Carol is giving him choice about involvement
jokes.punchline: His genuine affection for ex-wife who left him for a woman
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's complex feelings. Sad-funny rather than laugh-out-loud.

jokes.text: I mean, presumably, the biggest part of your job is done.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Ross being 'involved' with the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Chandler's crude but logical observation about conception being complete
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler - inappropriately practical observation delivered with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Well, this is still ruined, right?
jokes.setup: Sounds of Joey clanging utensils, presumably at the lasagna with the ring
jokes.punchline: Joey's practical question about whether the food is still edible
jokes.explanation: Pure Joey - focused on food despite the emotional drama around him. Character-driven humor.

jokes.text: What's that curry taste? / Curry.
jokes.setup: Dad questioning taste of food
jokes.punchline: Mom's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Simple setup-punchline with obvious answer. Basic but gets mild reaction.

jokes.text: No, Mom. I don't have a restaurant. I work in a restaurant. / Well, they don't have to know that.
jokes.setup: Mom telling people Monica has a restaurant
jokes.punchline: Mom's casual attitude toward lying about Monica's actual job
jokes.explanation: Good mother character moment - casual dishonesty to make family look better.

jokes.text: Well, at least she had the chance to leave a man at the altar.
jokes.setup: Parents criticizing Rachel for leaving Barry at altar
jokes.punchline: Monica's bitter observation about her own lack of marriage opportunities
jokes.explanation: Excellent Monica self-deprecation - finding the worst possible silver lining. Sharp and character-specific.

jokes.text: Even when you were a kid, and you were chubby... and you had no friends, you were just fine. You'd read alone in your room. And your puzzles...
jokes.setup: Dad trying to compliment Monica's independence
jokes.punchline: Increasingly devastating 'compliments' about her lonely childhood
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - well-meaning parent delivering crushing 'compliments' with escalating damage.

jokes.text: And I read about these women trying to have it all, and I thank God... our little Harmonica doesn't seem to have that problem.
jokes.setup: Mom continuing to 'compliment' Monica
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment about Monica's lack of ambition, plus wrong name
jokes.explanation: Great parent character work - devastating put-down disguised as compliment, plus getting child's name wrong.

jokes.text: [Long awkward silence after Ross reveals Carol's pregnancy and lesbian relationship]
jokes.setup: Ross finally tells parents about Carol being lesbian and pregnant
jokes.punchline: Extended uncomfortable silence as information sinks in
jokes.explanation: Well-timed awkward silence. The comedy is in the length of the pause and what it implies.

jokes.text: You're a twin? / Oh, yeah. We don't speak. / She's like this high-powered, driven, career-type. / What does she do? / She's a waitress.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing her ambitious twin sister
jokes.punchline: Twin has same job as Phoebe despite being described as high-powered
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. Perfect setup with Phoebe's perspective on what makes someone 'high-powered.'

jokes.text: Well, no, although I did have an imaginary friend... who my parents actually preferred.
jokes.setup: Being asked about not having sibling rivalry as only child
jokes.punchline: Parents preferred his imaginary friend over him
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler line - perfect self-deprecating humor with dark undertone about neglectful parents. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Well, you may want to steer clear of the word 'dumped.'
jokes.setup: Rachel asking for advice from someone recently dumped
jokes.punchline: Ross's correction about harsh terminology
jokes.explanation: Mild callback humor with Ross's sensitivity about relationship terminology.

jokes.text: Or, you know, hey, I'll go down there, and I'll give Barry back his ring. And you can go with Carol and Susan to the OB-GYN.
jokes.setup: Rachel and Ross both dreading their respective confrontations
jokes.punchline: Rachel's suggestion they swap uncomfortable situations
jokes.explanation: Logical character moment showing both are equally uncomfortable with their situations.

jokes.text: Quack, quack... quack, quack, quack. / Ross? / That opens my cervix.
jokes.setup: Machine making duck sounds at doctor's office
jokes.punchline: Carol's clinical explanation of uncomfortable medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Basic medical awkwardness humor. Gets reaction but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I dumped him. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Barry asking how Rachel is doing
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt confession and Barry's underwhelmed response
jokes.explanation: Callback to Ross's advice about avoiding 'dumped.' Barry's casual response is the real joke.

jokes.text: As in 'Mouse'? / As in my grandmother. / Still, you say 'Minnie,' you hear 'Mouse.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of baby name 'Minnie'
jokes.punchline: Ross's logical observation about Disney association
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor about name association. Ross being practical about baby names.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You went on our honeymoon alone? / No. / I went with Mindy.
jokes.setup: Rachel horrified Barry went on their honeymoon
jokes.punchline: Even worse - he went with her maid of honor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - bad situation made worse. Solid cringe comedy setup.

jokes.text: You got plugs. / Careful, careful. They haven't quite taken yet.
jokes.setup: Rachel noticing Barry's hair transplant
jokes.punchline: Barry's defensive response about his hair plugs
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy about male vanity. Gets a reaction but predictable.

jokes.text: But you hate sticking your finger in your eye. / Not for her.
jokes.setup: Rachel noting Barry got contact lenses despite hating them
jokes.punchline: Barry's admission he changed for new girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Good relationship observation about compromises people make for love they wouldn't make before.

jokes.text: Helen Geller? / I don't think so. / Hello, it's not gonna be Helen Geller.
jokes.setup: Ross suggesting his grandmother's name for baby
jokes.punchline: Lesbian couple rejecting his family name
jokes.explanation: Basic family name conflict. Sets up the longer naming argument sequence.

jokes.text: That's funny. Really? I don't remember you making any sperm.
jokes.setup: Susan claiming equal parental rights to baby naming
jokes.punchline: Ross's crude but logical point about biological contribution
jokes.explanation: Good Ross frustration moment. Awkward but logical argument delivered bluntly.

jokes.text: And we all know what a challenge that is.
jokes.setup: Ross boasting about his sperm contribution
jokes.punchline: Susan's sarcastic comeback about male reproductive effort
jokes.explanation: Good comeback line. Susan holding her own in the argument with sarcasm.

jokes.text: I don't know, but I think it's about to attack the Enterprise.
jokes.setup: Everyone looking at sonogram image
jokes.punchline: Chandler's Star Trek reference about unidentifiable blob
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that's character-appropriate for Chandler. Relatable sonogram joke.

jokes.text: You know, if you tilt your head to the left and relax your eyes... it kind of looks like an old potato.
jokes.setup: Everyone trying to see baby in sonogram
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's unhelpful visual comparison
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe - unhelpfully honest observation that deflates the moment. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: I just hope they have his old hairline and your old nose.
jokes.setup: Rachel wishing Barry and Mindy well on phone
jokes.punchline: Her petty curse disguised as well-wishes
jokes.explanation: Excellent petty revenge line. Perfect Rachel character moment - sweet on surface, vindictive underneath. Great callback structure.

jokes.text: Okay, I know it was a cheap shot, but I feel so much better now.
jokes.setup: Rachel's petty comment to Mindy
jokes.punchline: Her honest self-assessment and satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good character button - Rachel's self-awareness about her pettiness makes it more endearing.
